message:
feat(providers): honor CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR / CODEX_HOME + keychain fallback (#6)

Makes caut usable alongside side-by-side accounts and on macOS where
the Claude Code CLI stores OAuth credentials in the Keychain rather
than in a file.

src/providers/claude/mod.rs
- get_claude_dir() now checks CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR first, matching the
  knob Anthropic's Claude Code CLI uses to relocate its config.
  Empty / whitespace-only values fall through to the ~/.claude
  default so a shell-set-but-blank variable doesn't break discovery.
- has_local_config() additionally checks the macOS Keychain under
  service "Claude Code-credentials" when no file-based config is
  present. Previously "caut doctor" reported `Auth missing! Run:
  claude auth login` on macOS even when the CLI was fully logged in,
  because OAuth credentials on that platform live exclusively in
  the Keychain. The keyring crate is already a dependency so this
  is a zero-new-dep fix.
- Keychain probe is a no-op on non-macOS (const fn returning false).

src/providers/codex/mod.rs
- get_codex_dir() now checks CODEX_HOME first. Empty values fall
  through to ~/.codex. Fixes the observed behavior where
  CODEX_HOME=~/.codex-alt caut usage still read ~/.codex.

Tests:
- Each provider test module gets a serial-lock'd pair of tests:
  one asserting the env var wins, one asserting empty values fall
  back to the default. Mutex guards protect against parallel test
  invocations racing on process-global env state.

Scope note: the multi-dir config-driven discovery
(config.toml-driven, producing one row per (provider, account))
from the issue's third bullet is not in this commit. The env-var
support covers the standard side-by-side-account pattern users
already use with the upstream CLIs, and the Keychain fallback
closes the macOS gap. Multi-dir enumeration is additive and can
follow once this lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
